title: Non-determinism violation detection
purpose: Complete the violation-detection path that makes Aion safe: when replayed workflow code issues a command that does not line up with recorded history, the Resolver must catch it at the point of mismatch and fail the workflow loudly with a typed error, never silently producing a wrong Resolution or corrupting state. This is the single most safety-critical behaviour in the cluster and is held to dedicated test coverage.
task: Fill in the Resolver's Mismatch arm so a cursor Mismatch becomes a NonDeterminismError carrying the workflow id, the cursor's current sequence position, the expected command shape, and the found recorded-event shape. Wire the deterministic-failure consequence: a violation causes the workflow to fail deterministically, recording exactly one WorkflowFailed (via the Recorder from AD-002) classified as a non-determinism failure. Add the dedicated determinism-violation test file driving a recorded history that deliberately diverges from the replayed command stream. Test against InMemoryStore.

requirements.title: Mismatch -> typed NonDeterminismError
requirements.spec: WHEN the HistoryCursor reports a Mismatch for an issued Command, THE Resolver SHALL raise a NonDeterminismError carrying the WorkflowId, the cursor's current sequence position, the expected command shape (family + correlation key), and the found recorded-event shape. THE Resolver SHALL NOT return any Resolution for that Command.
acceptance: resolve(command) returns Err(NonDeterminismError{..}) when the cursor reports Mismatch.
The error's expected field names the issued Command's family and CorrelationKey; the found field names the recorded event's family and key.
The error's seq equals the cursor position at the point of mismatch.
There is no code path where a Mismatch yields Ok(Resolution) (CO10) — a test asserts the error is returned, not a guessed Resolution.

requirements.title: Deterministic workflow failure on violation
requirements.spec: WHEN a NonDeterminismError is raised during replay or live execution, THE SYSTEM SHALL fail the workflow deterministically by recording exactly one WorkflowFailed (via the Recorder) carrying a non-determinism failure classification, and SHALL NOT record more than one terminal failure for the same violation.
acceptance: A function (e.g. fail_on_violation) takes the Recorder and the NonDeterminismError and records a single WorkflowFailed whose WorkflowError reflects the non-determinism classification.
Calling it is idempotent at the call site (it is invoked once per violation; a guard or single call-site ensures one terminal event).
A test seeds a workflow, triggers a violation, invokes the failure path, reads history, and asserts exactly one terminal WorkflowFailed event with the non-determinism classification.
The WorkflowError variant/message used for the non-determinism case is documented so AE surfaces it consistently.

requirements.title: Dedicated divergence test suite
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a dedicated test that drives a recorded history which deliberately diverges from the replayed command stream and asserts the typed NonDeterminismError with the correct expected and found shapes.
acceptance: tests/determinism_violation.rs builds a recorded history (over InMemoryStore) and replays a command stream that diverges in two distinct ways: (a) wrong family (workflow issues a timer where history recorded an activity) and (b) wrong correlation key (workflow issues Activity(1) where history recorded Activity(0) next / a signal of the wrong name).
Each case asserts Err(NonDeterminismError) and asserts the expected and found descriptors and the sequence position.
The test is a real runtime test (not #[ignore], not env-gated) and passes under cargo test.
A control case in the same file replays a matching command stream and asserts no violation, proving the detector does not false-positive.

boundaries: SHALL NOT weaken detection with tolerance or heuristic matching — detection is exact family + key comparison at the cursor position (CO10).
SHALL NOT swallow, log-and-continue, or retry past a violation.
SHALL NOT define the WorkflowError type itself (that is aion-core AC-004) — it selects/constructs the appropriate existing variant for the non-determinism case.
verification: cargo test -p aion passes, including tests/determinism_violation.rs.
Grep confirms no Mismatch arm returns Ok(Resolution) and no violation path is swallowed.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
